abstracted_pattern: Eligibility approval (PI signature) MUST occur prior to Randomization/IRT entry.
Ophthalmology Assessment (OCT/Retinal) -> Required for Safety + Eligibility. Needs Specialist Report attachment.
12-lead ECG -> Requires original machine printout + exact timestamp + PI signature for clinical significance.
Patient eDiary Training performed after diary issuance -> Compromises primary endpoint data integrity.
scope: All Trials utilizing ePRO / Daily Diaries

abstracted_pattern: Q: Can randomization proceed if pregnancy test is undocumented? A: No. WOCBP require strict negative result verification pre-dose to mitigate teratogenic risk.
scope: All Trials involving IP exposure to WOCBP
exclusions: Trials enrolling exclusively male or surgically sterile populations

abstracted_pattern: If protocol contains Investigational Product (IP) dosing -> Blueprint MUST generate IP Administration Log + IP Accountability Log.
Addition of a new conditional lab requirement -> Requires Blueprint update, Source Worksheet versioning, and Delegation/Training log updates.
Infectious Disease Screening Pattern: Must verify symptom onset window (e.g., <48 hours) prior to randomization to ensure viral load viability.
Index Patient Pattern: Requires dedicated ID linkage field to associate the primary infected subject with downstream household contacts.
Household Contact Pattern: Contacts must have their own ICF and longitudinal symptom tracking logs, linked to the Index Patient ID.
Late Arrival Contact Pattern: Contacts entering the household >24h post-index-randomization MUST NOT be enrolled or dosed.
Respiratory Swab Pattern: Nasopharyngeal/Oropharyngeal swabs require strict collection timestamp + anatomical site verification + storage temperature log.
exclusions: Trials using only standard serum labs

abstracted_pattern: Local vs Central Lab Testing Pattern: Using local lab for primary viral shedding endpoint instead of Central Lab invalidates efficacy data.
SARS-CoV-2 Discontinuation Pattern: If rapid test is positive for exclusion virus (e.g. SARS-CoV-2 in Flu trial), IP must be stopped immediately.
scope: Acute Viral Trials with differential diagnosis exclusions

abstracted_pattern: Symptom Tracking Pattern: Requires a 4-point categorical scale (None/Mild/Moderate/Severe) for upper respiratory symptoms updated BID (twice daily).
Temperature Diary Pattern: Must log device type (oral/tympanic) and require exact timestamp for every fever reading.
Endpoint Transmission Pattern: If a household contact becomes symptomatic, they must be converted to an Index Patient trajectory or withdrawn based on protocol specific logic.
Remote Visit Pattern: Telehealth visits MUST explicitly log the method of contact (Phone/Video) and verify subject identity.
Early Discontinuation Pattern: If subject discontinues, the Early Termination (ET) visit procedures MUST be executed in lieu of the next scheduled visit.
Age-Specific Follow-up Pattern: Pediatric subjects require age-adjusted vital sign ranges and weight-based dosing logs.
Study Drug Within Time Window Pattern: First dose MUST be administered within a strict window (e.g., 2 hours) of Randomization/IRT assignment.
Specimen Chain-of-Custody Pattern: Central lab shipments require documented courier waybills and ambient/frozen temperature verification prior to dispatch.
Temperature Excursion Pattern: If storage temps fall out of protocol range, IP must be immediately quarantined and blocked from dispensing.
Quarantine Decision Pattern: Quarantined drug MUST NOT be released back to active inventory without explicit written sponsor/medical monitor approval.
Shipment Receipt Pattern: Must document receipt date, physical condition, and logger temperature history during transit prior to placing in active inventory.
Accountability Reconciliation Pattern: Log must capture Dispensed - Returned = Amount Used, with mandatory reason code for any missing unit.
Drug Dispensing Pattern: Dispensing requires real-time double-check of Subject ID, IRT dispensation confirmation, and expiry date.
Unblinded Access Pattern: Unblinded pharmacist MUST NOT share dispensing logs, IP assignment, or physical vial characteristics with the blinded clinical team.
Drug Return Pattern: Subjects must return empty, partial, and unused containers; site must log exact count returned.
Drug Destruction Pattern: Site MUST NOT destroy IP locally without written sponsor authorization and a fully completed accountability log.
Sponsor Notification Pattern: Temperature excursions and missing IP must trigger mandatory sponsor notification within 24h.
Pharmacy Deviation Pattern: Dispensing IP to the wrong subject or dispensing expired IP constitutes a critical protocol deviation requiring immediate IRB/Sponsor alert.
Inventory Mismatch Pattern: Discrepancies between IRT expected stock and physical stock must halt dispensing until reconciled.
Temperature Device Failure Pattern: If primary logger fails, backup min/max data must be reviewed; IP must be quarantined pending sponsor review.
Pharmacy Audit Readiness Pattern: All dispensing, excursion, and destruction logs must have wet/e-signatures from the delegated pharmacist.
Collection Window Pattern: Blood/Urine collection MUST occur strictly within the protocol-defined window (e.g., +/- 15 mins of PK timepoint).
Processing Window Pattern: Specimen processing (e.g., centrifugation, stabilization) must commence within strict time-to-processing limits from exact collection time.
Centrifugation Pattern: Must strictly adhere to specified RPM/G-force and duration. Ambient vs. refrigerated centrifuge requirement must be verified.
Aliquoting Pattern: Log must capture exact number of primary and backup aliquots generated, volume per aliquot, and corresponding barcode/tube IDs.
Storage Pattern: Specimens must be transferred to required temperature (e.g., -80C) within the max allowable time post-processing. Deviations invalidate the sample.
Chain of Custody Pattern: Must log every handoff from phlebotomist to processor to storage to courier. Timestamps and actor IDs are mandatory.
Shipment Pattern: Must verify dry ice weight, ambient gel packs, airway bill number, and inclusion of manifest prior to courier handoff.
Shipment Delay Pattern: Delays exceeding 24h for ambient or 48h for dry-ice shipments require proactive sponsor notification and lab alert.
Shipment Excursion Pattern: Samples arriving at central lab out-of-temp must trigger immediate query and sponsor QA review prior to analysis.
Lost Sample Pattern: Courier loss of specimen requires immediate PI notification, sponsor alert, and assessment for subject recollection viability.
Recollection Pattern: Protocol-mandated recollection (due to hemolysis or loss) requires an unscheduled visit log and new subject consent if not covered by original ICF.
Lab Reconciliation Pattern: Routine reconciliation of site shipping manifests vs. central lab accessioning reports must occur to detect mislabeled/missing aliquots.
Sample Destruction Pattern: Local destruction of biological specimens requires written sponsor authorization unless strictly dictated by site SOP.
Freezer Excursion Pattern: Site -80C freezer failure triggers immediate specimen transfer to backup, quarantine, and sponsor temperature logger review.
Specimen Deviation Pattern: Any deviation in collection tube type, processing time, or storage temp is a critical lab deviation impacting endpoint validity.

Deviation Adjudication Escalation: Any biospecimen deviation impacting timing, stability, or identity must be quarantined by the Site, queried by the Central Lab, evaluated for safety by the Medical Monitor, and ultimately adjudicated for analysis usability by the Sponsor/SAP team. Sites never unilaterally discard samples unless dictated by biological hazard SOPs.

Document Hierarchy Resolution: For biospecimens, the Protocol governs Scientific Intent (what/when), the Lab Manual governs Operational Execution (how/where), and the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) governs Data Usability (impact). In conflicts of physical handling, the Lab Manual overrides the Protocol until the Sponsor issues a formal clarification.

CRA Forensic SDV Trigger: Systematic timestamps mismatches or missing ALCOA+ elements (e.g., unsigned processing logs) trigger mandatory 100% Source Data Verification (SDV) audits by the Monitor, potentially escalating to a Note to File (NTF) or a Site-level CAPA to prevent FDA 483 observations.

Specimen Loss Financial Withholding: Unusable, lost, or majorly deviated biospecimens frequently trigger contract withholding clauses (Site Payment Denial) since the Sponsor did not receive scientifically valid endpoint data, despite the clinical visit having physically occurred.

Systemic Error Trend Flag: Repeated identical deviations (e.g., recurrent temperature excursions during shipping, recurrent hemolysis) by the same staff member must trigger an automated trend analysis, resulting in temporary procedural suspension, retraining (Training CAPA), and Monitoring Plan escalation.
Any query unresolved for >14 days elevates site risk. Queries >30 days trigger CRA escalation to PI. Queries >45 days risk financial withholding and DB lock delay.
Safety or Eligibility queries must be resolved within 48h. Failure triggers immediate Medical Monitor / Clinical Trial Manager escalation and blocks enrollment.
Isolated transcription errors or missing non-critical data. Generates an EDC query or SDV action item. Requires source correction or NTF.
Missed primary endpoints, incorrect IP dispensing, or unreported SAEs. Triggers immediate protocol deviation logging, Site PI meeting, and sponsor notification.
Ineligible subject randomized, IP given without consent, or fraud. Triggers immediate Clinical Hold at site, FDA/IRB notification, and For-Cause Audit.
A pattern of >3 identical major deviations, or any critical finding. Mandates formal Root Cause Analysis (5 Whys) and documented Corrective/Preventive Action plan within 15 days.
Post-CAPA implementation, if the same deviation recurs within 90 days, the CAPA is deemed ineffective. Escalates to Site Disqualification risk.
Source data modified without initial/date, or missing timestamps on critical endpoints. Triggers 100% SDV requirement for that subject and potential data exclusion.
High staff turnover, unresolved queries >50, or recurrent ALCOA+ issues. Shifts site from Risk-Based Monitoring to 100% targeted On-Site Monitoring.
PI failing to sign lab reports, failing to review SAEs, or delegating medical tasks to unqualified staff. FDA 483 risk. Requires immediate Sponsor intervention.
Procedure performed by staff not on DOA log, or after DOA end date. Nullifies the data point. Requires major deviation report and PI retraining.
Staff executing protocol without documented protocol training or current GCP/IATA. Freezes staff from study activities until documentation is secured.
Site non-responsiveness to CRA action items >30 days. Escalates to Lead CRA, then CTM, then formal Sponsor Warning Letter to PI.
Major deviations, un-SDV'd data, or open queries block visit payment milestones. Repeated compliance failures can terminate site contract for cause.
Combined presence of late SAE reporting, missing ICFs, and PI oversight failure. Highly likely to trigger a For-Cause FDA BIMO inspection.

Automated Missing Data Prediction: Engine scans source documents upon save. If a required ALCOA+ element (e.g., signature, timestamp, AE stop date) is missing, VIP flags the coordinator with a 95% probability of a future CRA Query, demanding correction before EDC transfer.

CRA Finding Interceptor: Engine detects systemic delays in source documentation (late entries) or unsigned lab reports. It alerts the PI/Manager that a Major CRA Finding is imminent, triggering a preemptive internal SDV review and Note to File (NTF) generation before the monitor arrives.

Risk Trajectory Alert: Engine computes approaching visit windows, expiring IATA training, and pending screening labs. Generates a 'Deviation Risk Score'. If risk >80%, it triggers a hard stop notification to the CRC to execute the visit/training immediately, averting a Protocol Deviation.

Continuous FDA Readiness Score: Engine continuously evaluates the gap between completed procedures and missing compliance anchors (e.g., unfiled ICFs, missing DOA signatures). Outputs an 'Audit Failure Probability'. A score >0% mandates immediate administrative lockdown.

ClinIQ Payment Risk Integrator: Engine maps unresolved queries, potential deviations, and missing endpoints directly to the Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) milestones. Outputs the exact dollar amount ($) of 'Revenue at Risk', framing clinical compliance strictly as a financial imperative for Site Management.

External EDC Cross-Firing Logic Guard: Engine flags data combinations that typically trigger proprietary Sponsor EDC custom edit checks. Generates a risk signal and a pre-export checklist for manual coordinator review. Does not block EDC export by default. An admin-configured hold is optional.
uncertainty_note: Third-party EDC logic is proprietary and may change without notice.

abstracted_pattern: ISF / eTMF Sync Alert: Tracks expiration of site-level regulatory documents (CVs, 1572s). Generates a regulatory readiness warning and a monitoring risk signal. Triggers PI/Regulatory escalation if a critical document is expired. Does not block EDC export automatically.
uncertainty_note: Some Sponsors may accept grace periods for certain document renewals.

abstracted_pattern: Pan-Site Coordinator Overload: Aggregates minor errors across multiple unrelated studies for a single user to generate a workload risk signal. Triggers a manager review recommendation and inputs into the staffing risk dashboard. Does not auto-reassign workload.
uncertainty_note: Coordinator burnout is subjective; metrics do not perfectly map to individual stress thresholds.

abstracted_pattern: Sponsor-Specific CAPA Alignment: When a trend triggers a root-cause analysis, Engine outputs the raw 5-Whys logic but flags that the final export must be manually formatted to the proprietary CRO template.
uncertainty_note: Sponsor templates update frequently and cannot be deterministically mapped.

abstracted_pattern: PI Interview Preparedness: Engine detects if PI has merely signed off on alerts vs actively resolving safety queries. Generates a PI readiness risk signal, outputs a suggested prep checklist, and issues a QA/admin scheduling recommendation. Does not force meetings automatically.
uncertainty_note: PI may have offline discussions not captured in EDC audit trails.

abstracted_pattern: Contractual Holdback Projection: Modifies cash flow forecasts based on configured CTA parameters. If the CTA budget is unavailable, indicates 'payment risk unknown'. Never invents dollar amounts or percentages.
uncertainty_note: CTAs contain complex qualitative milestones that defy linear mathematics.

abstracted_pattern: Time-Motion Query Burden: Provides a workload estimate based on a configurable task duration model and confidence score. Does not assume exact minutes unless specifically configured by administration.
uncertainty_note: Time-motion estimates vary wildly based on CRC experience level.

abstracted_pattern: Communication Latency Tracking: Monitors time-to-response on Sponsor/Medical Monitor queries or emails based on configurable SLA thresholds. Generates a sponsor confidence risk signal and an escalation recommendation. Does not classify the site as high-risk automatically.
Mandates explicit confirmation that a physical object (IP bottle, biospecimen tube) has been visually verified to match the documented ledger. Prevents blind trust in EDC data.
uncertainty_note: Cannot physically count objects via UI.

abstracted_pattern: Triggers immediate Quarantine/Finding when the stated ledger count (e.g., 20 pills) contradicts the physically verified count (18 pills). Triggers PI/Sponsor deviation escalation.
uncertainty_note: Assumes human physical count is accurate.

abstracted_pattern: Requires two distinct authenticated users to sign off on physical inventory changes (e.g., IP destruction) to prevent single-actor fraud or counting errors.
uncertainty_note: Depends on DOA mapping.

abstracted_pattern: Enforces timestamp tracking of physical custody handoffs (e.g., Pharmacy to CRC to Patient). Documented handoffs without physical signatures flag a CoC violation risk.
uncertainty_note: Handoffs happen physically outside UI.

abstracted_pattern: AI may flag abnormal values but strictly defers to PI judgment. Outputs {abnormality_detected:true, ai_adjudication_allowed:false, medical_authority:'PI'}.
uncertainty_note: AI cannot practice medicine.

abstracted_pattern: Blocks finalizing a safety-related visit until the PI explicitly marks an AI-flagged abnormal value as Clinically Significant (CS) or Not Clinically Significant (NCS).
uncertainty_note: None. Regulatory requirement.

abstracted_pattern: Any lab/ECG/vital outside protocol range generates a mandatory PI review task. Prevents 'passive' sign-off.
uncertainty_note: Requires accurate lab range limits.

abstracted_pattern: Explicit intelligence limit: If a question requires medical interpretation (e.g., 'Is this AE related to IP?'), VIP explicitly states it cannot answer and defers to PI/Sponsor.
uncertainty_note: Absolute boundary of AI.

abstracted_pattern: If no CTA data is present, outputs 'financial impact unknown — CTA/ClinIQ required'. Prevents hallucination of dollar figures.
uncertainty_note: Financial logic decoupled from clinical logic.

abstracted_pattern: Highlights clinical actions that typicall trigger payment delays (e.g., query aging >30 days) but refuses to calculate exact penalties without CTA mapping.
uncertainty_note: Qualitative risk only.

abstracted_pattern: Strict separation of concerns. Vilo OS identifies the clinical event; ClinIQ performs the financial calculation.
uncertainty_note: API Integration Required.

abstracted_pattern: VIP will never assume a standard 20% holdback. All holdbacks must be explicitly configured or queried from an external financial API.
uncertainty_note: Prevents false site revenue projections.

abstracted_pattern: Limits the frequency of popups/alerts for the same event. If a user dismisses a LOW risk alert, VIP suppresses identical alerts for X hours to prevent fatigue.
uncertainty_note: Risk of missing cumulative errors.

abstracted_pattern: Classifies events into INFO, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL, HARD_STOP. Only HIGH and above generate immediate UI interruptions; others are batched to a digest.
uncertainty_note: Subjective classification boundary.

abstracted_pattern: Repeated minor administrative issues (e.g., a known typo format in an external lab) are suppressed from UI popups and shifted silently into a Trend Dashboard.
uncertainty_note: Trend could mask a worsening problem.

abstracted_pattern: Ensures Site Directors/PIs only receive notifications for CRITICAL or HARD_STOP events (e.g., Eligibility Failure, 48h Safety Query), sparing them from administrative noise.
uncertainty_note: PI might prefer to see all data.

abstracted_pattern: If a CRC has >50 active tasks, the engine dynamically suppresses LOW/MEDIUM alerts and batches them to avoid triggering immediate psychological burnout.
uncertainty_note: Depends on accurate task volume calculation.
